identity.short_name: Probability of always-true statements in a triangle/circle/reflection configuration (9 cards)

problem_spec.summary: In an acute triangle, a line through the circumcenter defines points on two sides and a circle through a vertex. A free point on the third side defines a second intersection point with that circle. A reflection across a fixed side creates perpendicularity/midpoint invariants. Among a finite list of candidate statements, determine which are universally true, then compute a uniform probability and an encoded integer output.
givens: ABC is an acute triangle with circumcenter O.
A line \ell through O meets AB and AC at interior points D and E.
\omega is the circumcircle of triangle ADE.
K is chosen on open segment BC with K not on AO, and line AK meets \omega again at L\neqA.
M is the reflection of O across line AC.
F is the foot from O to line AB.
There are 9 fixed candidate statements; one is chosen uniformly at random; success means it is true for every admissible (\ell,K).
ask: Compute p=q/r in lowest terms and output 100q+r.

parameter(s).name: triangle_shape
parameter(s).purpose: ensures standard circumcenter/foot behavior and avoids degeneracy.

parameter(s).name: point_on_BC
parameter(s).purpose: determines L as second intersection of AK with \omega.

parameter(s).name: statement_set
parameter(s).purpose: determines which claims are tested for universality and the probability denominator.

invariant(s).name: collinearity_from_intersection_definition
invariant(s).statement: D and E lie on \ell and \ell passes through O, so D,O,E are always collinear.

invariant(s).name: concyclicity_from_circle_definition
invariant(s).statement: \omega is the circumcircle of A,D,E and L lies on \omega, so A,D,E,L are always concyclic.

invariant(s).name: collinearity_AKL
invariant(s).statement: L is defined as the second intersection of line AK with \omega, hence A,K,L are always collinear.

invariant(s).name: reflection_ac_perpendicular_bisector
invariant(s).statement: Reflecting O across AC makes AC the perpendicular bisector of OM; thus OM ⟂ AC and N=OM∩AC is the midpoint of OM.

invariant(s).name: universality_is_boolean_per_statement
invariant(s).statement: Each statement is either true for all admissible (\ell,K) or not; the count of universally true statements is well-defined.

essential_assumption(s): D and E are interior points so \omega is a nondegenerate circle.
K is chosen so that L exists as a distinct second intersection (AK not tangent to \omega at A).
Triangle is acute so circumcenter and perpendicular feet behave in the usual Euclidean way.

uniqueness_certificate(s).statement: The set of universally true statements is determined by proving a subset holds for all configurations and giving a single admissible counterexample for each remaining statement. Therefore the number of universally true statements, the probability p, and the encoded output 100q+r are unique.

technique(s).name: prove_universal_statements_by_unpacking_definitions
inputs: incidence definitions of D,E,\omega,L and reflection definition of M
technique(s).purpose: certify which statements are tautologically or structurally always true.

technique(s).name: prove_reflection_claims
inputs: M as reflection of O across AC
technique(s).purpose: derive OM ⟂ AC and midpoint property for N.

technique(s).name: disprove_nonuniversal_statements_by_coordinate_counterexample
inputs: freedom to choose an acute triangle and admissible \ell,K
technique(s).purpose: exhibit one explicit configuration where remaining statements fail.

technique(s).name: compute_probability_from_count
inputs: number of universally true statements and total statements

theorem(s).name: Reflection across a line gives perpendicular bisector
theorem(s).role: If M is the reflection of O across line AC, then AC is the perpendicular bisector of OM.

theorem(s).name: Circle through three noncollinear points is unique
theorem(s).role: Justifies defining \omega as circumcircle of triangle ADE.

theorem(s).name: Tangency criterion (radius ⟂ tangent)
theorem(s).role: A line is tangent to \omega at L iff it is perpendicular to the radius from center(\omega) to L.

theorem(s).name: Concyclicity check via circle equation / equal power
theorem(s).role: Supports coordinate verification that four points are not concyclic in a counterexample.

closest_first_principle_concept_applied: Use rigid definitions and reflection invariants to prove universality; use a single explicit counterexample to refute non-universal claims.

critical_trick(s): Identify a core set of statements that are always true purely from definitions (collinearity/concyclicity) and reflection invariants.
Use one explicit coordinate configuration to falsify all remaining candidate statements at once.
Convert the count into a uniform probability and compute 100q+r.

library(s).name: sympy
library(s).reason: exact coordinate geometry (circumcenters, intersections, circle equations) to verify counterexamples without floating error.

library(s).name: numpy
library(s).reason: optional numerical sanity checks (not required for exact certification).

task(s).name: coordinate_counterexample_verification
task(s).reason: compute D,E,\omega,L,M,N,F for a chosen triangle and test concyclicity/tangency claims mechanically.

task(s).name: randomized_sanity_sampling
task(s).reason: sample several admissible (\ell,K) to confirm only the proven universal statements persist.
